name: BuildingType
javadoc:
/**
 * Encapsulates data common to all instances of a particular kind of
 * {@link Building}, such as the number of workplaces, and the types
 * of goods it produces and consumes.
 */

members.name: BuildingType
members.javadoc: /** * Creates a new BuildingType instance. * * @param id The object identifier. * @param specification The Specification to refer to. */

tags.name: specification
tags.description: The Specification to refer to, not null

members.name: getLevel
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Get the level of this BuildingType.
 *
 * @return The building level.
 */

members.name: getWorkPlaces
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the number of workplaces, that is the maximum number of
 * Units that can work in this BuildingType.
 *
 * @return The number of work places.
 */

members.name: getUpkeep
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the amount of gold necessary to maintain a Building of
 * this type for one turn.
 *
 * @return The per turn upkeep for this building type.
 */

tags.content: per turn upkeep for this building type.
tags.description: The per turn upkeep for this building type.

members.name: getPriority
members.javadoc:
/**
 * The consumption priority of a Building of this type. The higher
 * the priority, the earlier will the Consumer be allowed to
 * consume the goods it requires.
 *
 * @return The consumption priority.
 */

members.name: getNoAddReason
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the reason why a given unit type can not be added to a * building of this type. * * @param unitType The UnitType to test. * @return The reason why adding would fail. */

tags.description: The reason why adding would fail.

members.name: canAdd
members.javadoc: /** * Can a unit of a given type be added to a Building of this type? * * @param unitType The UnitType to check. * @return True if the unit type can be added. */

tags.content: if the unit type can be added.
tags.description: True if the unit type can be added.

members.name: getType
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Gets the type of the building type, which is trivially just this
 * object.
 *
 * @return This.
 */

members.name: getUpgradesFrom
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the BuildingType this BuildingType upgrades from. * * @return The BuildingType that upgrades to this one. */

tags.description: The BuildingType that upgrades to this one.

members.name: getUpgradesTo
members.javadoc: /** * Get the BuildingType this BuildingType upgrades to. * * @return The BuildingType to upgrade to from this one. */

tags.content: BuildingType to upgrade to from this one.
tags.description: The BuildingType to upgrade to from this one.

members.name: getFirstLevel
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the first level of this BuildingType. * * @return The base BuildingType. */

members.name: isAutomaticBuild
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Is this building type automatically built in any colony?
 *
 * @return True if this building type is automatically built.
 */

tags.content: if this building type is automatically built.
tags.description: True if this building type is automatically built.

members.name: addProductionType
members.javadoc: /** * Add a production type to this building type. * * @param productionType The ProductionType to add. */

members.name: getAvailableProductionTypes
members.javadoc: /** * Get the production types provided by this building type at the * current difficulty level. * * @param unattended Whether the production is unattended. * @return A list of ProductionTypes. */

members.name: getAvailableProductionTypes
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the production types available at the current difficulty * level. * * FIXME: TileType.getAvailableProductionTypes(boolean) uses the * GameOptions.TILE_PRODUCTION option. Should we implement a * corresponding one for BuildingTypes? * * @param unattended Whether the production is unattended. * @param level The production level (NYI). * @return A list of ProductionTypes. */
members.code: /** * Gets the production types available at the current difficulty * level. * * FIXME: TileType.getAvailableProductionTypes(boolean) uses the * GameOptions.TILE_PRODUCTION option. Should we implement a * corresponding one for BuildingTypes? * * @param unattended Whether the production is unattended. * @param level The production level (NYI). * @return A list of ProductionTypes. */ public List getAvailableProductionTypes(boolean unattended, String level) { return productionTypes.stream().filter(pt -> pt.getUnattended() == unattended && pt.appliesTo(level)).collect(Collectors.toList()); }

members.name: getProducedGoodsType
members.javadoc: /** * Gets the type of goods produced by this BuildingType. * * @return The produced GoodsType. */

members.name: isDefenceType
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Is this a defence-related building type?  Such buildings
 * (stockade et al) are visible to other players.
 *
 * @return True if this is a defence related building.
 */

tags.content: if this is a defence related building.
tags.description: True if this is a defence related building.

members.name: canProduce
members.javadoc: /** * Can a tile of this type produce a given goods type? * * @param goodsType The GoodsType to produce. * @param unitType An optional UnitType that is to do * the work, if null the unattended production is considered. * @return True if this tile type produces the goods. */

tags.name: unitType
tags.content: An optional UnitType that is to do the work, if null the unattended production is considered.
tags.description: An optional UnitType that is to do the work, if null the unattended production is considered.

tags.content: if this tile type produces the goods.
tags.description: True if this tile type produces the goods.

members.name: getBaseProduction
members.javadoc: /** * Get the base production of a given goods type for an optional * unit type. * * @param productionType An optional ProductionType to use, * if null the best available one is used. * @param goodsType The GoodsType to produce. * @param unitType An optional UnitType that is to do * the work, if null the unattended production is considered. * @return The amount of goods produced. */

tags.name: productionType
tags.content: An optional ProductionType to use, if null the best available one is used.
tags.description: An optional ProductionType to use, if null the best available one is used

tags.name: unitType
tags.content: An optional UnitType that is to do the work, if null the unattended production is considered.
tags.description: An optional UnitType that is to do the work, if null the unattended production is considered

members.name: getPotentialProduction
members.javadoc: /** * Get the amount of goods of a given goods type the given unit * type could produce on a tile of this tile type. * * @param goodsType The GoodsType to produce. * @param unitType An optional UnitType that is to do * the work, if null the unattended production is considered. * @return The amount of goods produced. */

tags.name: unitType
tags.content: An optional UnitType that is to do the work, if null the unattended production is considered.
tags.description: An optional UnitType that is to do the work, if null the unattended production is considered.
